data.narrative:
Dexterity, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Samir Menon and co-founders, currently carries a $1.65B post-money valuation following $291M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment relies on a peer universe of nine companies: one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers in the robotics sector, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Collaborative Robotics, Fairmat, and GrayMatter Robotics.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage advancement, yields $3.1B. Peer-median benchmarking across five same-sector, same-stage comparables produced a $500M implied value, with the peer set ranging from $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in robotics valuations, with 1X Technologies at the high end ($10.0B) and GrayMatter Robotics at the low end ($300M).

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $3.6B (high), with a median of $1.8B. Dexterity's current $1.65B valuation sits near the low-to-median bound of this range. Key uncertainties include the stage-progression multiplier assumption and the relevance of 1X Technologies as a comp given its substantial valuation outlier status. The wide dispersion in peer valuations suggests meaningful uncertainty around appropriate benchmarking within the sector.
